page: Microsoft Azure D2ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Ddsv7 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The server features 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes a 480 MB L3 cache.

This instance includes 118 GB of local SSD storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. It does not feature any GPU accelerators. The underlying virtualization is managed by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The CPU architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), alongside hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES and SHA_NI.

Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2268.08 and a strong single-threaded gzip compression rate of 30.79 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory subsystem performance are generally limited. The multi-core stress-ng score is 2300.17, and the uncached memory write bandwidth is relatively low at 11.86 GB/sec. Database operations and static web serving throughput also fall into lower performance tiers, with Redis SET operations achieving 4.06 million operations per second.

Given its hardware profile, the D2ds_v7 offers a balanced, entry-level resource density. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that require dedicated CPU execution and local scratch space but do not demand high memory bandwidth or massive parallel processing. Appropriate workloads include lightweight web serving, basic single-threaded data processing, development environments, and small-scale utility applications.
description: The Microsoft Azure D2ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by a dedicated 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 2 vCPUs (1 physical core with hyper-threading), 8.0 GB of RAM, and 118 GB of local SSD storage. The CPU features a large 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. Benchmark data shows average single-core performance, highlighted by a strong single-threaded gzip compression speed of 30.79 GB/sec, though multi-core and memory write bandwidth metrics are comparatively weak. Lacking GPU acceleration, this instance is a qualitatively cost-effective option for lightweight, single-threaded tasks. It is best suited for general-purpose workloads, including small-scale web serving, development environments, and basic utility applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 118 GB of local SSD storage, designed for entry-level workloads and development environments.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel processors, integrated local SSD storage, and advanced cryptographic instruction sets.

8.0 GB of system memory and 118 GB of local SSD storage
Supports AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX)
Best suited for lightweight web serving and development environments
